observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the following proposed adjusting journal entries in the GF Books to correct the misstatements noted:

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant agreed to cause the transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-MOOE, QRF and lump sum appropriation to the Special Trust Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in accordance with IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances especially from those who are already separated/retired from the service or no longer connected with the Municipality, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant set up the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of each year as required under Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records and ensure that delinquent RPT and SET as at year-end are recorded in the books of the Municipality under the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their respective records, including inquiry with the creditor bank to determine the outstanding balance of the Municipality based on the bank records as at a given cut-off date, and reflect the accurate balance of the Loans payable account in the Financial Statements at each reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following courses of actions:
The Municipal Treasurer make representations to the identified payee on the checks that became stale; and
Pending action of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant-Designate make the proposed adjusting entry below to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the subsidiary ledgers and schedules of the above-mentioned accounts to the Audit Team for audit. Henceforth, promptly submit these documents together with the financial statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 41 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to hasten the preparation of the BRS in order to ascertain the correctness of the bank balances appearing in the financial statements. Henceforth, they agreed to prepare and submit the BRS on time to the Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to:
reconcile the recorded taxes withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance and cause the remittance to BIR once reconciled or identified; and
conduct monthly reconciliation of the Due to BIR account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted balances.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to reconcile the recorded PhilHealth premium contributions withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance per employee and cause the immediate remittance to the PhilHealth once these are reconciled or identified.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct monthly reconciliation of the account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted or unreconciled balances.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to reconcile the recorded mandatory contributions withheld and loan amortization payments against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance per employee or contractual personnel and cause the immediate remittance to the HDMF once these are reconciled or identified.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct monthly reconciliation of the account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted or unreconciled balances.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to reconcile the recorded GSIS premium contributions withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance per employee and cause the immediate remittance to the GSIS once these are reconciled or identified to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and to enable the member as well as the beneficiaries to avail of benefits from the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the liquidation reports including the supporting documents thereto. Further, we recommended that the all documents pertaining to the recorded monthly transactions be submitted at once following the reglementary period set forth in the auditing regulations.

We recommended that the LCE in coordination with the LDRRM Officer incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be sourced from the unexpended LDRRM fund of the previous years together with the current year’s allocation for inclusion in the AIP to be approved by the Local Sanggunian and LCE upon recommendation of the LDRRMC pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended and Management, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat, agreed to submit to the Audit Team all perfected contracts/agreements, purchase orders and their supporting documents within the reglementary period of five days.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to:
Apply for or adjust the fidelity bond coverage, as warranted, for all AOs with the BTr, ensuring that the bond amount is proportionate to their respective cash accountabilities; and
Conduct periodic reviews of the fidelity bond sufficiency to ensure continuous compliance with the Public Bonding Law and related BTr issuances.
The Municipal Accountant accounts for all the borrowing costs attributable to the acquisition of assets and prepare adjusting entries if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required complete financial statements reports/schedules for our verification within the reglementary period as prescribed under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Section 41 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the auditor the municipal accounts for the period November to  December 2017.  Henceforth, the municipal accounts should be submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to  Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairman, Members and Secretariat to training/s on procurement to professionalize  the procurement procedures and standards of the LGU to ensure transparency and adherence to the Procurement Law.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat update the Municipality’s APP by including items that the agency wants to procure but are not initially consolidated therein and have it approved by the Mayor as head of the procuring entity before buying goods and materials not included in the APP. Logically, only those items that have the corresponding funds/appropriations shall be consolidated into the Supplemental APP.

We also recommend that Management submit document/s that will provide proof that the subject procurements were backed up with valid appropriations to avoid disallowance of the transactions.

We recommend that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to cause the submission of the lacking documents to avoid disallowance in audit.  Henceforth, the BAC shall automatic declare disqualification of any bidder who fails to submit the eligibility and technical documentary requirements of any public bidding conducted.

We recommend that the LGU adopt the mode of procurement  recommended by the BAC preferably public bidding pursuant to the 2016 Revised Implemented Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order not to invalidate their procurements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC of the Municipality to diligently observe the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and see to it that supporting documents of its procurements are in order in order to avoid speculations of irregularities from the public.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit the lacking documents in order to facilitate complete evaluation of the procurement otherwise the transaction shall be suspended in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee to cause the submission of the lacking documents to avoid disallowance in audit.  Henceforth, the BAC shall automatically declare disqualification of any bidder who fails to submit any of the eligibility and technical documentary requirements of any public bidding conducted.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Health Officer and BAC Chairman and Members to submit a written explanation on non-compliance with the applicable provisions of EO 49.  Henceforth, the concerned municipal officials should see to it that any requisition for drug products must be accompanied by the following document/s to avoid suspension in audit:
Certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I; or
For drugs not listed in the PNDF Vol. I, a written request with corresponding justification addressed to the Head of the National Drug Policy Office who may approve or disapprove the request.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit her explanation in writing and regularize the transactions by signing the ObRs under the SEF otherwise the disbursements will be suspended in audit.  Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer should refrain from implementing measures/procedures which are not supported with written guidelines from the COA or agencies authorized by law.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit her explanation in writing and regularize the transactions by signing the ObRs under the SEF otherwise the disbursements will be suspended in audit.  Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer should refrain from implementing measures/procedures which are not supported with written guidelines from the COA or agencies authorized by law.

We  recommend that the Municipal Mayor shall enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the budgetary limitations as provided for under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act (RA) 7160 and Section 5.3.3 of Local Budget Circular No. 75 to ensure that the LGU shall have a balanced budget.
We recommend that the current municipal Local School Board revisit the provisions of Section 98 of RA 7160 and reorganize the composition of the LSB.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit the required monthly report otherwise the authorized sanction allowable by law shall be fully implemented.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provision of Section 2(Q) of COA Circular No. 92-389 by stamping PAID all disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents, as a means in preventing double payment which will result on loss of government funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer   should refrain from approving transactions  and issuing checks for any disbursement vouchers that lack the required certification and signature to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal  Accountant  to trace the difference between the bank and accounting records  to establish the correct and accurate Cash in Bank balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the land account by recording the correct valuation which is the acquisition or historical cost of the lots to ensure that they are accurately and fairly presented in the financial statements.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the registration of all lots acquired by the LGU to  legalize, provide public notice of its ownership and stop any future claim against them.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant  to book the cash advances granted to the Disbursing Officer other than for payrolls under the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to trace the reason for the negative/abnormal balance of the Cash Local Treasury account so that the corresponding adjustment could be made and present the correct and accurate balance as of year-end pursuant to Section 111(2) of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference of the affected accounts in the Post-closing Trial Balance, Statement of Financial Position and statement of Financial Performance to establish the correct and accurate balances in the financial statements as prescribed under Section 111 (2) of PD 1445.

It is recommended that the concerned municipal officials submit the foreign travel authority duly signed by the Undersecretary for Local Government to avoid audit disallowances on all the claims made for the travel, otherwise the liable officials/employees be required to refund immediately the claimed per diems totaling P169,493.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit  to the auditor the required financial statements within the prescribed period as provided for under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010 - 001,                                                       Section 41 (2) and 122 of PD 1445 and  COA Circular 2015-009 otherwise, the allowed sanctions will be implemented pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to reconcile the negative/abnormal balance of the Cash Local Treasury account so that the corresponding adjustment could be made and present the correct and accurate balance as of year-end pursuant to Section 111(2) of PD 1445.
We recommend that the BAC Chairman submit the lacking documents and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order not to invalidate their procurement.

We recommend that the BAC of the Municipality diligently observe the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and see to it that supporting documents of its procurements are in order in order to avoid speculations of irregularities from the public.
We also recommend that the BAC submit the lacking documents in order to facilitate complete evaluation of the procurements.

We recommend that the BAC Chairman and members submit the required documents in order to avoid disallowance of the transaction.
We also recommend that  the Municipal Accountant refrain from certifying any disbursement voucher that lacks required supporting documents.  In case of disallowance, she becomes one of the persons liable.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit the required report/s pursuant to  RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-02 otherwise, the allowable sanctions authorized by law shall be implemented.

We reiterate our  prior years’ audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile each other’s records, maintain PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each asset category and properly classify/identify the PPEs by maintaining property cards to ascertain their existence, valuation and completeness.

We reiterate our prior years’ recommendation that the Accountant should be directed to trace back the source document of accounts which remained idle for more than ten (10) years and without supporting documents  information or data that support its validity.  If found without valid and legal basis write off or dropping of accounts from the books may be made after complying the necessary documents required for such writing off.
